summary: This is an extremely brief post where Spolsky references his earlier advice against signing non-compete agreements and provides a link to an external example illustrating why. The post serves as a follow-up validation of his prior stance, using a real-world case as evidence. It contains no substantive argument of its own beyond connecting these two references.
key_points: Spolsky reiterates his stance that developers should never sign non-compete agreements
He uses a real-world example to validate his earlier advice
money_quotes: Remember how I said you should never sign non-compete agreements? Here's why.
key_insight: Non-compete agreements are harmful to employees, and real-world cases consistently demonstrate why developers should refuse to sign them.
